meta.siteDescription: The reading app you close. Build a calm personal library, become the reader you meant to be — no feeds, no noise.
meta.homeTitle: Readen · The reading app you close
meta.ogTagline: Become the reader you meant to be.

hero.title: Become the reader you meant to be.
hero.tagline: Build a library you're proud of — calm, beautiful, and entirely yours.
hero.subline: No feeds. No noise. Just the books that shaped you, kept on a shelf that feels real.

library.title: Every book you own, in one calm place.
library.body: Readen keeps your whole reading life on one shelf: what you want to read, what you are reading, and what you have finished.
library.point1: Three shelves keep it honest: want to read, reading, read.
library.point2: Your page is always saved. Pick any book back up right where you stopped.
library.point3: Rediscover the books you already own instead of buying another one you will not open.

manifesto.quote: Your reading life belongs in a room of its own, not in a feed.
manifesto.sub: So we built an app you close, not one that keeps you.

experience.title: A life measured in books, not notifications.
experience.body: Open Readen and the noise stops. No feed to scroll, no numbers to chase. Just your shelf, your pace, and the quiet pride of a life spent reading.
experience.pullQuote: Close the app. Open a book. That is the point.

identity.title: A sanctuary, not a feed.
identity.body: The best products respect a moment. Readen gives your reading life a private room, craft you can feel, and books chosen by people worth listening to.
identity.pillar1Body: No social feed, no streak pressure. Your library is a quiet room that belongs to you alone.
identity.pillar2Body: Covers that behave like books. Glass shelves. The small physical details that make you want to open the app — and then close it to read.
identity.pillar3Body: Discover books because a founder, scientist, or artist said they mattered — not because an algorithm needed your attention.

moment1.title: One book. One light. No notifications.
moment1.body: The best reading moments are small. Readen is built for the quiet hour before the day starts — when the only thing competing for your attention is the next page.

moment2.title: Pick up exactly where you left off.
moment2.body: Your page is always saved. Your shelf always looks the same. Come back tonight to the same calm room, not a feed that moved on without you.

story.quote: I used to buy books I never opened. Readen didn't fix that with guilt — it fixed it with pride. Opening my shelf feels good. I finished three books last month for the first time in years.
story.name: Elena M.
story.label: San Francisco

features.title: A library that works like you read.

scan.title: Scan
scan.headline: From barcode to shelf in a second.
scan.body: Point your camera at any ISBN. The book lands on your shelf with title, cover, and metadata. Done.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.headline: Read in sessions. Keep the rhythm.
sessions.body: A timer, a page slider, and a literary quote when you close the book. Your weekly streak takes care of the rest.

goals.title: Goals
goals.headline: See your reading life.
goals.body: Hours read, books finished, days with a session, pages turned. Your whole reading year at a glance.

mock.quote: A reader lives a thousand lives before he dies.

widgets.title: Your book follows you home.
widgets.body: A widget on your Home Screen, a live session in the Dynamic Island, a literary quote that changes with the book you're reading. Readen stays close — and never asks for your attention.
widgets.quote: A reader lives a thousand lives before he dies.
widgets.point1Body: Your current book, its cover, and your progress — one tap and you're back on the page.
widgets.point2Body: Start a session and a quiet timer keeps you company until you close the book.
widgets.point3Title: A quote that knows your book
widgets.point3Body: The large widget pairs your progress with a literary quote matched to what you're reading, refreshed daily.

discover.title: Curated by readers who shape the world.
discover.body: No algorithmic feed. Every book in the Discover catalog is there because a founder, scientist, artist, or investor said it mattered.

review1.quote: Finally a reading app that feels like a library, not a feed.
review1.name: James R.

review2.quote: The covers alone are worth it. Everything else is a bonus.
review2.name: Sofia L.

review3.quote: Calm, beautiful, and it respects the books.
review3.name: David K.

review4.quote: I built my whole shelf in two minutes. It just works.
review4.name: Priya N.

review5.quote: Sessions changed how I finish books.
review5.name: Marcus T.

review6.quote: The curated catalog is genuinely good.
review6.name: Anna W.

onboarding.title: Build your shelf in two minutes.
onboarding.body: Answer two questions and Readen sets up your starter shelf. No empty-library cold start.
onboarding.step1Body: Philosophy, sci-fi, strategy, memoirs. Choose what pulls you in.
onboarding.step2Body: Readen matches books recommended by remarkable people in your world.
onboarding.step3Body: A starter shelf, standing on glass, ready before you even sign in.

download.title: Your library is waiting.
download.body: Free on iOS and Android. No ads, no feeds. Just your books — beautifully kept.

footer.tagline: The reading app you close. A calm home for the books that shaped you.

about.title: A digital sanctuary for readers.
about.story: Reading apps became feeds: noisy, social, optimized for engagement instead of the book. I built Readen because I missed the feeling of a personal library. A shelf that is yours. Books that stay where you put them. A calm place to read, track, and rediscover what matters to you.
about.value1: No feeds, no pressure. Your library is a quiet room.
about.value2: Covers that feel physical. Shelves that feel real. Details matter.
about.value3: No ads, no data selling. Your reading life belongs to you.
about.numbers: {books} curated books · {people} world-class readers · {recommendations} recommendations · iOS and Android
about.roadmap: Readen is just getting started. A web discover catalog, book clubs, and a signed-in web experience are on the horizon, always in service of the same idea: a personal library you are proud of.
about.contactIntro: Press, partnerships, or just hello. Reach us at

what.a: Readen is a personal book library for iOS and Android. Curate your shelf by status: want to read, reading, read. Scan barcodes, track sessions, set goals, and discover a curated catalog of books recommended by remarkable readers.

platforms.q: Which platforms is Readen available on?
platforms.a: Readen is available on iOS and Android. A web version of the app is planned for a future release.

free.a: Yes. Readen is free to use with no ads and no data selling.

scan.a: Open the Scan tab and point your camera at any book's ISBN barcode. Readen looks up the book metadata and adds it to your shelf instantly.

data.q: Where does book data come from?
data.a: Book metadata is sourced from ISBNdb and other catalog providers. Our curated discover catalog is built from recommendations by world-class readers.

account.a: You can build a starter shelf before signing in. An account (via Google or Apple) is only needed to save and sync your library across devices.

offline.a: Your library is available offline. Reading sessions queue locally and sync when you are back online.

delete.q: How do I delete my account?
delete.a: Go to Settings → Account in the app and tap Delete Account. Your data will be permanently removed.

recommendations.a: Readen's discover catalog is curated by People: founders, investors, scientists, artists. Not an algorithmic feed. Each recommendation comes from a real reader.

contact.a: Email us at hello@readen.co and we will get back to you.

notFound.title: This shelf is empty.
notFound.body: The page you are looking for does not exist.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service govern your use of Readen, a personal book library application operated by Readen ("we", "us"). By using Readen, you agree to these terms.
terms.useBody: Readen is provided for personal, non-commercial use. You may not reverse engineer, scrape, or misuse the service. You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account.
terms.contentBody: Your library data belongs to you. By using Readen, you grant us the limited right to store and sync your data to provide the service.
terms.terminationBody: You may delete your account at any time through Settings → Account in the app. We may suspend accounts that violate these terms.
terms.liabilityBody: Readen is provided "as is" without warranties. We are not liable for any indirect damages arising from your use of the service.
terms.contactBody: Questions about these terms? Email hello@readen.co.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Readen respects your privacy. This policy explains what data we collect, how we use it, and your rights.
privacy.collectBody: Account information (via Google or Apple sign-in), your library data (books, reading sessions, goals), and anonymized usage analytics. We do not sell your data.
privacy.useBody: To provide and improve Readen: sync your library, track sessions, and show relevant book metadata. Analytics are privacy-first (no cookies, no cross-site tracking).
privacy.thirdPartyBody: We use Supabase (auth and database), ISBNdb (book metadata lookups), and privacy-first analytics. Each provider processes data according to their own policies.
privacy.deleteBody: You can delete your account in-app via Settings → Account. All associated data is permanently removed.
